An important amplifier of the authority principle is the perception of credibility. In fact, research conducted around the world found that the single most powerful authority communicator behavioral science has ever uncovered is the credible communicator. But credibility has two components, perceived expertise and perceived trustworthiness. Let's examine each starting with expertise, which of course we've already considered to an extent in terms of our true knowledge, experience and credentials relevant to the issues at hand. But now, let's consider how to best present ourselves in a meeting or to an audience that might not be aware of that evidence.
